questions.question: If MAT is coded as OCV, how will MAN be coded in that same language?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by exactly 2 positions in the English alphabet.
questions.trick: Note the position gap between the first letter of the word and the first letter of its code.

questions.question: Find the odd one out among the following numbers: 25, 36, 101, 49
questions.explanation: 25, 36, and 49 are perfect squares. 101 is not a perfect square.
questions.trick: Memorize squares up to 30 to instantly spot discrepancies.

questions.question: A can complete a piece of work in 30 days while B can complete the same work in 58 days. If they work together, in how many days will they complete the work?
questions.explanation: Work = 30 * 58 = 1740. Rate = 30 + 58 = 88. Time = Work / Rate = 19.77 days.

questions.question: If an article is bought for Rs. 500 and sold at a profit of 50%, what is the selling price?
questions.trick: Calculate 10% of CP, multiply, and add it conceptually.

questions.question: What will be the Simple Interest on a principal amount of Rs. 8000 at an interest rate of 7% per annum over 4 years?
Find the next number in the series: 14, 24, 34, 44, 54, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows an Arithmetic Progression with a constant difference of 10. So, 54 + (10) = 64.
questions.trick: Always check the difference between the first and second terms and verify across the series.

questions.question:
Statements:
1. All Apples are Fruits.
2. All Fruits are Foods.

Which conclusion logically follows?
questions.explanation: If A is a subset of B, and B is a subset of C, then A must be a subset of C completely.
questions.trick: Draw a venn diagram: Small circle A inside Medium B inside Large C.

questions.question: A train travels at an average speed of 94 km/hr. How much distance will it cover in 3 hours?
questions.trick: Basic multiplication rule: D = S * T.
